ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of customer interaction (e.g., Customer, Technical Support) */,
  "location_index" INTEGER /* Index representing the geographical location of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer's details were created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer's details were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_location_index FOREIGN KEY ("location_index") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_windows" (
  "service_window_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service window */,
  "tw_start" INTEGER /* Start time window for service */,
  "tw_end" INTEGER /* End time window for service */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer requesting the service */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the service window (e.g., scheduled, in-progress, completed) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes about the service window */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_window_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_windows_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographical location */,
  "x_coord" REAL /* X-coordinate for the location */,
  "y_coord" REAL /* Y-coordinate for the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_times" (
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "service_time_fix" INTEGER /* Fixed service time estimate (in minutes) */,
  "toll_area" INTEGER /* Area or cost associated with the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service request */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer making the request */,
  "service_window_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated service window */,
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service type requested */,
  "request_status" TEXT /* Status of the service request (e.g., pending, in-progress, resolved) */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the resolution of the service request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_service_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_type_id") REFERENCES service_times ("service_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_requests_service_window_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_window_id") REFERENCES service_windows ("service_window_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_service_agents" (
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer service agent */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the customer service agent */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the customer service agent */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer service agent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("agent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_request_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service request assignment */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service request being assigned */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer service agent assigned to the request */,
  "assignment_status" TEXT /* Status of the assignment (e.g., assigned, in-progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_request_assignments_agent_id FOREIGN KEY ("agent_id") REFERENCES customer_service_agents ("agent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_request_assignments_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES service_requests ("request_id")
)
